hot_topics.topic: Samsung Unveils New Foldable Phones and Smartwatches
hot_topics.synthesis: Samsung has introduced its 2026 Galaxy Unpacked lineup, featuring new foldable smartphones and smartwatches. The range includes a Z Fold 8 Ultra with an invisible crease, a wider Z Fold 8, and a thinner Z Flip 8 that offers a more traditional smartphone feel. Despite increasing prices due to component shortages, these devices are available for pre-order and aim to enhance user experience with innovative designs.

hot_topics.topic: Arizona Primaries Dominated by 2020 Election Deniers
hot_topics.synthesis: Arizona's Republican primaries saw a sweep for several Trump-backed candidates who deny the 2020 election results, including Rep. Andy Biggs for governor. While this stance secured primary victories, political analysts suggest it could hinder Republican chances in the general election, as moderate voters may reject candidates tied to election denialism. These results set the stage for highly contested and potentially challenging general election campaigns in the swing state.

hot_topics.topic: Escalating US-Iran Tensions in Red Sea and Strait of Hormuz
hot_topics.synthesis: Tensions between the US and Iran are escalating, marked by continued attacks in the critical Red Sea waterway, potentially undermining diplomatic efforts for a ceasefire. Former President Trump has issued a threat to target Iranian infrastructure, including bridges and power plants, if attacks in the Strait of Hormuz persist. A US official also testified on the significant financial burden the conflict has placed on the United States, which has already cost $37.5 billion.

hot_topics.topic: Global Push to Ban Social Media for Children
hot_topics.synthesis: Countries worldwide are increasingly implementing or considering bans and restrictions on social media access for minors, aiming to safeguard children from online risks. France recently passed a law prohibiting individuals under 15 from social media, requiring platforms to verify user ages. This follows Australia's earlier ban in late 2025, reflecting a growing international effort to protect young users from cyberbullying, addiction, and exposure to harmful content.

items.title: Can cells think?
items.summary:
Inside the lab of a biologist who is seeking to understand intelligence by focusing on problem-solving, not neurons
- by Aeon Video
Watch on Aeon

items.title: Anthropic’s $1.5B copyright settlement approved; only 350 authors opted out
items.summary: A judge approved a $1.5 billion copyright settlement between Anthropic and authors, ending the largest class-action copyright case certified. While some authors opposed the settlement due to payout concerns, most did not opt out of the agreement.

items.title: Confusion swirls on source of diarrhea outbreak, but it’s still Taylor Farms
items.summary: Federal officials reaffirmed that iceberg lettuce from Taylor Farms remains the suspected source of a multi-state Cyclospora outbreak causing explosive diarrhea. This clarification followed confusion from a false positive test and vague recall information provided by Taylor Farms.

items.title: Let Tom Hiddleston be your guide to Pompeii's final day
items.summary: National Geographic's new three-part docuseries, "Pompeii: Out of Time," features actor Tom Hiddleston as a "time detective" exploring the lives of Pompeii's inhabitants before Mount Vesuvius erupted. Hiddleston's interest stems from his classics background and a prior role on Disney+'s "Loki."

items.title: Naked mole-rat queens use a chemical signal to suppress fertility in rivals
items.summary: Naked mole-rat queens maintain their eusocial colonies by suppressing the fertility of other females using a sophisticated olfactory signaling program, rather than through bullying. This discovery explains how queens control breeding in vast underground tunnel networks.

items.title: Nintendo says users voluntarily paid higher prices, have no right to tariff refunds
items.summary: Nintendo is seeking to dismiss a class-action lawsuit demanding it pass tariff refunds on to customers, arguing that purchasers voluntarily paid listed prices and have no legal entitlement to such refunds. The lawsuit alleges unjust enrichment and violation of consumer protection laws.

items.title: On the run for 20 years, most-wanted fugitive caught hiding as a biotech exec
items.summary: Ronald Fischer, a former anesthesiologist and one of Rhode Island's most-wanted fugitives, was arrested after more than 20 years on the run, during which he allegedly hid as a biotech executive under the alias Richard Graydon. Fischer had fled his 2005 sexual assault trial.

items.title: Range Rover answers the question: "What if we built a not-SUV?"
items.summary:
Range Rover provided flights from Washington, DC, to London and accommodation so Ars could see the RR GT, as well as some other things you can read about in early September. Ars does not accept paid editorial content.
WOLVERHAMPTON, England—One can argue about whether or not Rang…

items.title: Samsung unveils new Galaxy Z8 foldables and Galaxy Watches, available August 7
items.summary:
The rumors haven't left much to the imagination, but Samsung has unfurled its 2026 foldable lineup today. There are three models for the first time in Samsung's eight-gen foldable family, including the top-of-the-line Z Fold 8 Ultra. That phone is the successor to last year's
Gal…

items.title: Sony releases one last trailer for Spider-Man: Brand New Day
items.summary: Sony Pictures released a final trailer for "Spider-Man: Brand New Day," a new film intended to launch a fresh trilogy with Tom Holland as Peter Parker. The movie explores a world where Peter Parker is forgotten and faces a new invisible threat.

items.title: Ukrainian drones deliver robots directly into battle by sea and air
items.summary: Ukraine is employing innovative battlefield tactics using flying drone carriers and drone boats to deploy ground robots armed with weapons or explosives. These methods help bypass Russian defenses and conserve Ukrainian manpower by enabling remote assaults.

items.title: Unlimited AI tokens aren't unlimited after all as US Army burns through supply
items.summary: The US Army's Combat Capabilities Development Command faced token limits for its AI platform, Ask Sage, despite an earlier announcement of "unlimited tokens." The Army quickly exhausted its annual token supply within a month, raising questions about future access.

items.title: What happens when you try to chop a photon in half?
items.summary: Norwegian physicists explored what happens if a photon, an extended object rather than a point particle, is interrupted during reflection. This complex inquiry challenges the common understanding that photons cannot be divided.

items.title: Beluga whales relocated from closed Canadian marine park arrive in US
items.summary: Six beluga whales have been successfully relocated to the US from a Canadian marine park that closed due to an inability to care for them.

items.title: British woman jailed for blackmail after accusing banker of rape in Hong Kong
items.summary: Isabel Rose, a British woman, was jailed in Hong Kong after being convicted of blackmail and perverting the course of justice. She was found guilty of attempting to extort £100,000 from a UK banker whom she accused of rape.

items.title: Cocaine worth $370m seized in Liberia's biggest-ever drugs bust
items.summary: Liberian authorities, following a lengthy investigation into drug-trafficking networks, seized $370 million worth of cocaine in the country's largest-ever drug bust.

items.title: France passes law banning under-15s from social media
items.summary: The law will require social media platforms in France to verify all users age from January.

items.title: Gruesome images in D4vd hearing are 'worst nightmare' for teen girl's family
items.summary: A court hearing for singer D4vd revealed gruesome details, including orders for a body bag, chainsaws, and an inflatable pool, for the alleged disposal of a teen girl's body, causing distress to her family.

items.title: India's Modi government under growing pressure as 'cockroach' protests intensify
items.summary: India's Modi government is facing increased pressure as "cockroach" protests intensify, gaining momentum after the opposition Congress party demonstrated outside the Prime Minister's residence.

items.title: Iran war has cost US $37.5bn so far, Hegseth says, as Pentagon seeks billions more
items.summary: The hearing was disrupted by protests and a shouting match as Hegseth testified at a pivotal time in the war.

items.title: Japan temperatures soar over 40C, marking first 'cruelly hot day'
items.summary: Japan experienced its first "cruelly hot day" with temperatures soaring over 40C, a term introduced by the weather agency in April for such extreme heat.

items.title: Michelin-starred restaurateur faces jail for using ants as dessert garnish
items.summary: A Michelin-starred restaurateur in South Korea faces potential jail time for allegedly using approximately 49,000 ants in various dishes over four years.

items.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
items.summary: Daniel Siad, a modeling scout whose name appeared thousands of times in the Epstein files, was found dead in Paris. Siad had denied knowing the extent of Jeffrey Epstein's threats.

items.title: Police station opens at Hitler's birthplace in bid to rid site of Nazi link
items.summary: A police station has opened at Adolf Hitler's 17th-century birthplace in Braunau am Inn, Austria, following years of controversy regarding the site's association with the Nazi dictator. The move is part of an effort to neutralize any neo-Nazi symbolism.

items.title: Rebel Wilson cleared in defamation case brought by actress in her own film
items.summary: Actress Rebel Wilson was cleared in a Sydney court of defamation charges brought by Charlotte MacInnes, an Australian actress who accused Wilson of defaming her.

items.title: Tankers make sharp U-turns after Houthi shipping threat
items.summary: All of the ships were travelling to or from Saudi ports before changing course, ship-tracking data shows.

items.title: Tokyo urges men to wear shorts to work, but women say it's 'leg hair harassment'
items.summary: The Tokyo Metropolitan Government's initiative encouraging men to wear shorts and casual attire to work to combat heat has faced criticism, with some women describing it as "leg hair harassment."

items.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
items.summary: Donald Trump says the US will respond any time Iranian forces shoot at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Tunnel collapse in India kills 14 workers
items.summary: A tunnel collapse in India resulted in the deaths of 14 workers, with hazardous conditions hampering ongoing rescue efforts to reach others still trapped.

items.title: Ukrainian drones hit Russian online giant retailer Wildberries for second time
items.summary: Ukrainian drones reportedly struck logistics hubs belonging to the Russian online retailer Wildberries in the Krasnodar and Stavropol regions overnight for a second time.

items.title: US to announce deal allowing Saudi Arabia a nuclear programme
items.summary: The US is set to announce a deal that would permit Saudi Arabia to develop a nuclear program, a long-sought ambition for the kingdom. This agreement is expected to draw concern from Israel and some US lawmakers.

items.title: WhatsApp usernames could upset Somalia's anti-terror fight
items.summary: WhatsApp's upcoming feature allowing users to chat without revealing their phone numbers could complicate Somalia's anti-terrorism efforts.

items.title: Why Iran is risking so much over the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: None of the choices facing Iran’s leaders offer a clear path to victory, as the conflict with the US continues.

items.title: Zelensky sacks Ukraine's top army commander after days of protests
items.summary: Ukrainian President Zelensky dismissed Oleksandr Syrskyi as the country's top army commander following days of public protests. The dismissal came after backlash over the removal of popular Defence Minister Mykhailo Fedorov.

items.title: Greetings from Jerusalem, whose holy sites host some of the oldest colonies of nesting swifts
items.summary: Jerusalem's ancient holy sites, including the Western Wall, have served as nesting grounds for scythe-winged swifts for thousands of years.

items.title: Her son was killed by ICE at a traffic stop. She says she's still waiting for justice
items.summary: The mother of Ruben Ray Martinez, who was fatally shot by an ICE agent during a traffic stop two months into President Trump's second term, claims her family has yet to receive justice.

items.title: Independent autopsy "inconclusive" on cause of death for 18-year-old Nolan Wells in Mississippi
items.summary: An independent autopsy for 18-year-old Nolan Wells in Mississippi was inconclusive, leaving the cause of death undetermined pending further investigation, according to attorney Ben Crump.

items.title: Peptides get their regulatory close-up
items.summary: A panel of FDA advisers will convene to recommend whether specialized pharmacies should be permitted to compound popular wellness peptides.

items.title: States want to bring Medicaid behind bars. Federal changes make that harder
items.summary: States attempting to extend Medicaid coverage to incarcerated individuals face obstacles due to funding cuts and program changes mandated by President Trump's new budget law.

items.title: Trump has campaigned more so far in 2026 midterms than he did at this point in 2018
items.summary: President Trump has been campaigning more actively in the 2026 midterms than at the same point in 2018, having already rallied for a dozen candidates in competitive races.

items.title: Trump to attend dignified transfer of fallen soldiers. And, Hegseth testifies on Iran
items.summary:
Trump will attend the dignified transfer of U.S. service members killed in the Middle East. And, Pete Hegseth is requesting billions from Congress to help with the rising cost of the war in Iran.
(Image credit: Saul Loeb)

items.title: Trump-backed candidates, 2020 election deniers, win Arizona primaries
items.summary:
The primaries set the field for what are expected to be heated contests for Congress and governor in a swing state this fall.
(Image credit: Ross D. Franklin)

items.title: U.S. and Iranian attacks continue as tensions mount on a key waterway in the Red Sea
items.summary:
The attacks overshadowed diplomatic efforts in Pakistan, one of the conflict's mediators, where a senior Iranian official held talks potentially aimed at reviving a collapsed ceasefire between the United States and Iran.
(Image credit: Atta Kenare)

items.title: What forecasters expect this hurricane season, as Tropical Storm Bertha edges along
items.summary: Forecasters anticipate fewer Atlantic storms this hurricane season due to the El Niño weather pattern, but unusually warm ocean temperatures still pose a high risk of destructive storms.

items.title: Mark Lamb wins Arizona House primary despite sex scandals
items.summary:
A scandal-plagued House candidate powered by President Donald Trump’s endorsement won a messy primary Tuesday.
Mark Lamb, the former Pinal County sheriff, overcame a late push from self-funding candidate Daniel Keenan in Arizona’s 5th District. Lamb will enter November as the hea…

items.title: Rep. Andy Biggs wins GOP primary for Arizona governor
items.summary:
Trump-endorsed Rep. Andy Biggs has won the GOP primary to face Democratic Gov. Katie Hobbs in November, setting up a marquee governor’s race in one of America’s most competitive states.
Biggs, a former chair of the hardline House Freedom Caucus and a close ally of President Donal…

items.title: South Carolina Senate poll shows surprising frontrunner: Trey Gowdy
items.summary: A poll for South Carolina's special Senate election shows former Rep. Trey Gowdy as a formidable frontrunner despite his stated disinterest in running for the late Sen. Lindsey Graham’s seat. The poll also indicates that Trump's endorsement of Darline Graham only modestly shifts support.

items.title: Trump-backed Jay Feely wins GOP primary in Arizona’s battleground 1st District
items.summary:
Former Arizona Cardinals kicker Jay Feely cruised to victory in Arizona’s 1st District on Tuesday, one of the nation’s most competitive battlegrounds.
Feely, a close ally of President Donald Trump, leveraged Trump’s endorsement to topple former state Rep. Joseph Chaplik.
He’ll fa…

items.title: ‘It’s a stone around your neck’: Arizona Republicans once again nominate 2020 election deniers
items.summary:
To make it out of a Republican primary in Arizona, there’s one key litmus test: believing the 2020 election was stolen from Donald Trump.
Election deniers who played prominent roles in efforts to overturn the 2020 results triumphed in statewide Republican primaries Tuesday:
Rep.…

items.title: Apple teams up with Klarna to launch a lease-to-own program for iPhones, iPads, and Macs
items.summary: Apple has partnered with Klarna to introduce a lease-to-own program for its iPhones, iPads, and Macs, marking a significant shift in its hardware sales strategy amidst rising product prices.

items.title: Bucking EV slowdown, Sila raises $300M to expand battery materials factory
items.summary: Sila raised $300 million in funding to expand its factory for silicon-carbon anode material, aiming to produce enough to power over 100,000 electric vehicles, despite a general slowdown in EV growth.

items.title: Cascade raises $3.5M to help construction firms find and win projects
items.summary: Cascade raised $3.5 million in seed funding from investors including a16z Speedrun to develop a platform that assists construction firms in identifying and securing new projects.

items.title: Data centers expected to use 4x more electricity by 2035
items.summary: Projections indicate that new data centers built through 2033 could consume four times more electricity by 2035 than they do today, potentially matching India's current total electricity usage.

items.title: Einride bets $38M on EV charging as it scales electric trucking
items.summary: Einride, now a publicly traded company, invested $38 million to acquire a company and expand its EV-charging ecosystem, supporting its efforts to scale electric trucking operations.

items.title: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
items.summary: Google released three new Gemini models—3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber—but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro has raised questions about its broader AI strategy.

items.title: Instagram now lets you swap out the music in your old posts
items.summary: Instagram has introduced a new "Replace Audio" tool, allowing users to change the music in their existing posts at any time. This feature preserves the post's original engagement metrics like likes and comments.

items.title: Jack Dorsey is taking on Slack with Buzz, a group chat platform for teams and their AI agents
items.summary: Jack Dorsey has launched Buzz, a new workplace group chat platform designed to integrate both human teams and their AI agents into the same conversations.

items.title: Meta is testing an AI bedtime story app for people with no imagination
items.summary: Meta is reportedly testing an AI-powered bedtime story application designed to generate stories, aiming to automate a traditional human pastime.

items.title: OpenAI says Hugging Face was breached by its pre-release models
items.summary: OpenAI has claimed responsibility for a breach at Hugging Face, stating it occurred as a result of internal testing of its pre-release models.

items.title: Passionfroot raises $15M to expand its B2B creator marketplace to the US
items.summary: German startup Passionfroot secured $15 million in Series A funding, led by Insight Partners, to expand its B2B creator marketplace into the US market.

items.title: Synthesia’s AI training platform is moving beyond videos into live coaching
items.summary: Synthesia launched AI Roleplay Sessions, an enterprise training platform that allows employees to practice workplace conversations with AI avatars. The system provides feedback, scoring, and analytics to measure training effectiveness.

items.title: Tesla spins up robotaxi pilots in Orlando and Tampa ahead of Q2 earnings
items.summary: Tesla initiated robotaxi pilot programs in Orlando and Tampa, taking a cautious approach to network scaling ahead of its Q2 earnings report, despite CEO Elon Musk's previous ambitious promises.

items.title: The Anthropic-Physical Intelligence rumor roiling AI Twitter
items.summary: A rumor regarding a potential acquisition involving Anthropic and Physical Intelligence has stirred discussion on AI Twitter, set against a backdrop of aggressive acquisition activity by major AI companies in 2026.

items.title: The browser wars aren’t about search anymore — here are the best alternatives to Chrome and Safari
items.summary: This article provides an overview of alternative web browsers challenging the dominance of Chrome and Safari, indicating a shift in the focus of browser competition beyond just search capabilities.

items.title: These are the countries moving to ban social media for children
items.summary: Australia was the first country to issue a ban in late 2025, aiming to reduce the pressures and risks that young users may face on social media, including cyberbullying, social media addiction, and exposure to predators.

items.title: WhatsApp adds Apple CarPlay and Android Auto upgrades, iPad sign-ups, and more
items.summary: WhatsApp has introduced upgrades for Apple CarPlay and Android Auto, enabling hands-free messaging, calling, and access to contacts and call history directly from a car's screen. The platform also added iPad sign-up capabilities.

items.title: Bernard-Henri Lévy: History Is Not Dead. It Is Under Attack.
items.summary: Bernard-Henri Lévy responds to Niall Ferguson's "The Death of History" by asserting that history is under attack, rather than dead, due to the spread of antisemitism, Holocaust denial, and the unprecedented disinformation power of AI and digital farms.

items.title: How Fergie Chambers Built a Radical Funding Network and Landed in a Spanish Jail
items.summary: Fergie Chambers, an heir to the Cox Enterprises fortune, established the "Babochki Collective" to funnel his $250 million inheritance into anti-imperialist and far-left causes, including pro-Palestine activism. His network includes activists involved in protests and sabotage operations.

items.title: Is History Dead?
items.summary: Historian Niall Ferguson argues that AI and social media are overwhelming traditional historical truth-preserving institutions, spreading "anti-history" and making it harder to learn lessons from the past, particularly regarding antisemitism and genocide. Other historians weigh in on whether history is truly "dead" or merely facing new challenges.

items.title: I almost forgot Samsung’s Z Flip 8 was a foldable
items.summary:
Samsung's new Galaxy Z Flip 8 feels more like a regular phone than ever. It's thinner, lighter, and has a front screen you can do just about anything on. Whether that's a good or bad thing, I'm not quite sure yet.
I spent three hours with Samsung's newest flip phone last week, an…

items.title: Meta made its own AI detection system. It should have just used Google’s
items.summary: Meta introduced "Content Seal," an invisible watermarking technology for images generated by its new AI model, intended to flag deceptive AI content. Critics suggest it is less reliable and accessible than existing solutions like C2PA Content Credentials or Google's SynthID.

items.title: Microsoft is bringing original Xbox games to PC
items.summary: Microsoft is expanding Xbox backward compatibility by bringing original Xbox games to PC, starting with an early preview of four classic titles. These games will be available for purchase on PC and included with all Xbox Game Pass plans.

items.title: Philips’ new smart toothbrush shows you where you didn’t properly brush
items.summary: Philips' Next-Generation DiamondClean 9900 Prestige smart toothbrush uses a third-generation AI model to provide real-time brushing feedback. A glowing ring and touchscreen highlight areas needing more attention, aiming to improve brushing routines.

items.title: Samsung Galaxy Unpacked 2026: The 6 biggest announcements
items.summary: Samsung's annual summer event kicked off on Wednesday morning with the company unveiling a new lineup of watches and foldables. Following weeks of rumors and leaks, Samsung announced the Galaxy Z Fold 8 with a completely new design that's shorter and wider than last year's Fold,…

items.title: Samsung Unpacked 2026: all the news from the July foldable launch
items.summary:
Samsung revealed its newest slate of foldables at this summer’s Galaxy Unpacked, which took place in London. In addition to
a Galaxy Z Fold 8
that adopts a wider, passport-like form factor, Samsung showed off its new
Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra
with a crease you can barely see and
a re…

items.title: Samsung’s newest foldable finally feels Ultra
items.summary:
While we wait for Apple's
rumored foldable iPhone
, Samsung is polishing a design it already has on the market. I just spent three hours with my hands on the new Z Fold 8 Ultra, and all I can think about is how Samsung finally made the crease invisible.
Samsung says it achieved t…

items.title: Samsung’s wider Z Fold 8 feels just right
items.summary:
The new Z Fold 8 is squat and wide, but don’t hold that against it. | Photo: David Imel / The Verge
A year after overhauling its Z Fold phone with a radically thinner design, Samsung has changed things up again, this time altering the shape entirely. The Z Fold 8 is wider than an…
